steps.title: Identify what the blank must supply
steps.content: The sentence pivots: "But that doesn't mean Johnson never learned what other people thought of her plays." The blank must name the mechanism through which she did receive that feedback, despite being excluded from professional theater.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘an occasion for professional theater companies to put on plays.’: The passage says professional companies did not support Black women's plays — this directly contradicts the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘an opportunity for Johnson to get feedback on her plays.’: The passage describes gatherings where attendees read and discussed one another's work, including Johnson's. This is exactly the feedback mechanism the blank requires. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘a way for Johnson to learn about plays that were produced in other cities.’: Learning about other cities' productions is not mentioned in the passage and does not address what other people thought of her own plays. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘subject matter for future plays by Johnson.’: No support in the passage that the gatherings provided Johnson's future subject matter. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
